question.text: An investment adviser continues to operate without addressing a SEBI compliance notice for 45 days. Under Section 15EB of the SEBI Act, what is the maximum penalty that can be imposed for this continuing violation?

answer.text: Rs. 46 lakh (Rs. 1 lakh minimum + Rs. 1 lakh x 45 days)
Rs. 1 crore (the maximum cap applies regardless of the number of days)
Rs. 1.45 crore (Rs. 1 lakh minimum + Rs. 1 lakh per day x 45 days + additional fine)

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Under Section 15EB, while the penalty can accrue at Rs. 1 lakh per day, the maximum cap is Rs. 1 crore regardless of how many days the violation continues. Even though 45 days x Rs. 1 lakh = Rs. 45 lakh (which is below the cap), the question asks for the maximum penalty — which is always Rs. 1 crore. The per-day calculation of Rs. 45 lakh is within the cap, but SEBI can impose up to the maximum of Rs. 1 crore for the severity of the violation.
**The Trap:** Option A appears logical (simple multiplication) but ignores that SEBI has discretion to impose penalties beyond the per-day calculation, up to the Rs. 1 crore cap. The maximum penalty is always Rs. 1 crore, regardless of the arithmetic.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Penalty calculation questions are frequently tested in NISM Series X-A. Understanding the three-tier structure (minimum, per-day, maximum cap) is essential.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Never ignore a SEBI compliance notice. The per-day penalty clock starts ticking immediately, and SEBI has discretion to impose penalties up to the Rs. 1 crore cap based on the severity and duration of the violation.
